records.ety: [F. congeler, L. congelare, -gelatumn; con- + gelare to freeze, gelu frost. See Gelid.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Congealed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Congealing.]

defs: To change from a fluid to a solid state by cold; to freeze.
quotes: A vapory deluge lies to snow congealed.

defs: To affect as if by freezing; to check the flow of, or cause to run cold; to chill.
quotes: As if with horror to congeal his blood.

defs: To grow hard, stiff, or thick, from cold or other causes; to become solid; to freeze; to cease to flow; to run cold; to be chilled.
quotes: Lest zeal, now melted . . . Cool and congeal again to what it was.
